laws.title: Public Law 30-222 | Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court Establishment Act
laws.status_basis: The source text creates a new FISA Court and related procedures without any self-repeal or sunset, and the archive treats it as a live statutory note.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the \"Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court Establishment Act\"."
provisions.notes: Introductory material only.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose to establish the FISA Court for surveillance oversight.
provisions.evidence: "The purpose of this Act is to establish the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court"
provisions.notes: Policy statement, not a Code rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Establishes the FISA Court and its composition.
provisions.evidence: "There is hereby established a court to be known as the \"Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court\""
provisions.notes: The court is created as a statutory note at the closest subject-matter anchor.

provisions.text_summary: Sets surveillance-application requirements, oversight, operational framework, and scope of authority.
provisions.evidence: "The Attorney General or their designee shall submit applications"; "The FISA Court shall review applications"; "A process for appeals and reviews"
provisions.notes: No single positive-law section is identified.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is concise, on-topic for Title 50, and does not carry a quotedContent dump; the remaining issue is the Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain as a concise statutory note at /us/usc/t50/s1.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 30-223 | Municipal Transportation Act
laws.status_basis: The extracted file is a Google Drive download-error page rather than recoverable statutory text, and the chronology seed later lists PL 36-260 as repealing PL 30-223; the safest disposition is historical-only.

provisions.text_summary: Municipal transportation enactment preserved only as history.
provisions.evidence: "Google Drive - Can't download file"; the current XML note places the law at "/us/usc/t49/s101/b/6" as statutory history.
provisions.notes: Source text is unavailable in the extracted file, so no section-by-section reconstruction is possible.

current_implementation.detail: The extracted source file is a permission/download error page, so the statutory text cannot be re-derived from the supplied file; the existing note is historical-only.

recommended_actions: Retain only a concise historical note if a recoverable source is later found.
Do not invent missing statutory text from the error page.

laws.title: Public Law 30-224 | Vacancies and Acting Officer Reform Act
laws.status_basis: The act rewrites the acting-officer framework, including exclusivity, holdover, notification, and effective-date rules, and nothing in the source body self-repeals the enactment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the \"Vacancies and Acting Officer Reform Act\""; "The purpose of this legislation is to reform the process for appointing acting officers"
provisions.notes: Introductory matter only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals most of 5 U.S.C. subchapter III and declares conflicting prior law void.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal 5 U.S. Code subchapter III in its entirety except for sections 3341-3344, and treat conflicting prior law as repealed unless expressly excepted.
provisions.evidence: "5 U.S. Code Subchapter III shall be repealed in its entirety, except for § 3341-3344"
provisions.notes: This is an express textual repeal, not a note.

provisions.text_summary: Sets the acting-officer order and first-assistant rule.
provisions.exact_change: Rewrite the acting-officer rule to make the first assistant, and then the President's designated officer or employee, serve temporarily subject to the Act's limits.
provisions.evidence: "the first assistant to the office of such officer shall perform the functions and duties of the office temporarily in an acting capacity"
provisions.notes: Core operative amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Sets time limits, exclusivity, holdover positions, exclusions, notification rules, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "Sections 4 and 5 of this Act are the exclusive means"; "This Act shall become effective upon enactment"
provisions.notes: These provisions are uncodified governance rules.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note at Title 5 section 101 already tracks the acting-officer framework in baseline form; the remaining issue is the quotedContent dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain the existing baseline incorporation of the acting-officer framework at /us/usc/t5/s101.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 31-225 | Collaborative Officer Partnership and Safety (COPS) Act
laws.status_basis: The act establishes a general COPS framework, definitions, and effective-date/severability language without any later-law repeal in the supplied materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and severability.
provisions.evidence: "Collaborative Officer Partnership and Safety (COPS) Act"; "This Act shall be severable"
provisions.notes: Introductory and savings language only.

provisions.text_summary: Defines proscribed felonies, principal head, and cooperative agreement.
provisions.evidence: "The term \"Proscribed felony\" means"; "The term \"Cooperative agreement\" means"
provisions.notes: Definition material only.

provisions.text_summary: Creates the general COPS rules, cooperation standards, and enforcement framework.
provisions.evidence: "there shall be a department known as" is not present here; the act instead sets general enforcement and cooperation rules for officers and agencies.
provisions.notes: No clean positive-law rewrite target is identified.

provisions.text_summary: Implements general duties, reporting, and the effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be effective immediately upon passage"
provisions.notes: Retain as concise statutory-note material.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is on a plausible Title 46 anchor and the posture is correct for a note-only enactment, but it embeds a full quotedContent dump and a Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain the enactment as a concise statutory note at /us/usc/t46/s101.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 31-226 | Commerce and Labor Administrative Reform Act (CLARA)
laws.status_basis: The source body contains a broad Commerce and Labor reorganization, explicit repealments of prior public laws, and a direct repeal of 5 U.S.C. 101, with no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, findings, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be referred to by its short title"; "Congress makes and declares"
provisions.notes: Introductory material only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals a package of prior public laws.
provisions.target: Public Law 4-29; Public Law 12-80; Public Law 15-92; Public Law 17-126; Public Law 19-152; Public Law 25-183
provisions.exact_change: Repeal the listed prior public laws in their entirety.
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 4-29 shall be repealed, in its entirety"; "Public Law 25-183 shall be repealed, in its entirety"
provisions.notes: These are historical repeal records, not a positive-law Code rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Creates the Department of Commerce and Labor and transfers Commerce/Labor authorities and related title structure.
provisions.exact_change: Establish the Department of Commerce and Labor, allocate the Commerce and Labor departments and listed agencies into it, and apply the stated title-level restructuring across the cited titles.
provisions.evidence: "Under the Executive Branch there shall be a department known as the Department of Commerce and Labor"; "Title 15"; "Title 29"; "Title 35"; "Title 47"; "Title 51"; "Title 19"
provisions.notes: Broad structural rewrite with multiple determinable code targets.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 5 U.S.C. 101.
provisions.evidence: "5 U.S. Code § 101 shall be repealed in its entirety"
provisions.notes: This is a direct textual repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Sets government labor-management policy and enforcement rules.
provisions.evidence: "The government finds and declares that an effective collective"; "The provisions of this section shall become effective upon the"
provisions.notes: Programmatic policy language, not a clean positive-law section rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note at Title 5 section 101 already reflects the Commerce and Labor restructuring in baseline form; the remaining issue is the quotedContent dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain the existing baseline incorporation of the Commerce and Labor restructuring at /us/usc/t5/s101.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 31-227 | Foreign Affairs Representation and Treatment Statute (FARTS)
laws.status_basis: The source body is a generally applicable diplomatic statute with one explicit Code repeal and several uncodified mission, immunity, and procedure provisions, and no later-law repeal appears in the supplied materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be referred to as \"Foreign Affairs Representation and Treatment Statute\""; "This bill shall be effective upon passage"
provisions.notes: Introductory and savings language only.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose, general applicability, and repealment rules.
provisions.target: Public Law 19-147; Public Law 23-171; Public Law 3-17
provisions.exact_change: Treat the prior public-law repeals as historical enactment material.
provisions.evidence: "The purpose of this act is to amend existing US law"; "Pursuant to Public Law 1-1, codes listed in this act shall be applicable"
provisions.notes: No single positive-law section rewrite is identified here.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 8 U.S.C. 1102.
provisions.evidence: "8 U.S. Code § 1102 shall be repealed, in its entirety"
provisions.notes: This is the clear Code target in the source.

provisions.text_summary: Mission functions, accreditation, credentials, premises, protections, immunity, and waiver rules.
provisions.evidence: "The functions of a diplomatic mission consist"; "The premises of the mission shall be inviolable"; "The immunity of a Foreign Diplomat"
provisions.notes: These are uncodified diplomatic-structure rules best preserved as note material.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note at the Title 8 anchor already reflects the repeal baseline for 8 U.S.C. 1102; the remaining issue is the quotedContent dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain the existing baseline incorporation of the 8 U.S.C. 1102 repeal at /us/usc/t8/s1101/i/2.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 31-228 | Protecting Congressional Authority Act (PCAA)
laws.status_basis: The source body reinstates 2 U.S.C. 192 and adds contempt-of-Congress procedures without any self-repeal or later repeal in the supplied materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "Protecting Congressional Authority Act"; "This Act shall be severable"; "This bill shall be effective immediately"
provisions.notes: Introductory and timing language only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 16-112 and reinstates 2 U.S.C. 192.
provisions.exact_change: Reinstate 2 U.S. Code section 192 in full and treat the prior PL 16-112 repeal as historical repeal history.
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 16-112 shall be repealed, in its entirety"; "2 U.S. Code § 192 shall be reinstated in full"
provisions.notes: This is the operative Code change.

provisions.text_summary: Creates contempt-of-Congress rules and associated chamber procedures.
provisions.evidence: "Each chamber of congress shall possess the ability to hold an individual in contempt of Congress"
provisions.notes: General procedural language best retained as note material.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note at the Title 2 anchor already reflects the reinstated 2 U.S.C. 192 baseline; the remaining issue is the quotedContent dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain the existing baseline incorporation of the 2 U.S.C. 192 reinstatement at /us/usc/t2/s1.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 31-229 | Fiscal Year 25-1
laws.status_basis: The act is an appropriations measure with an immediate effective-date provision and a pay adjustment restriction, and it contains no self-repeal or sunset in the supplied source text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the \"Fiscal Year 25-1 Act\""; "This Act shall take effect immediately"
provisions.notes: Introductory appropriations language only.

provisions.text_summary: Appropriations and emergency-fund availability rules.
provisions.evidence: "The following sums are hereby appropriated"; "Any amount designated in this Act as an emergency requirement"
provisions.notes: Appropriations generally do not become permanent Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Freezes congressional pay raises by referencing 2 U.S.C. 4501.
provisions.evidence: "no adjustment shall be made under section 601(a) of the Legislative Reorganization Act of 1946 (2 U.S.C. 4501)"
provisions.notes: The source imposes a spending/personal-compensation restriction but does not supply a clean rewrite target.

provisions.text_summary: Agency appropriations and fiscal-year budget items.
provisions.evidence: "prioritizes defense, law enforcement, and homeland security"
provisions.notes: Budget line-items belong outside permanent positive-law text.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is concise and appropriate for an appropriations measure at a Title 31 anchor; the remaining issue is the Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain a concise statutory note at /us/usc/t31/s101.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 32-230 | Federal Executive & Senior Leadership Pay Standardization Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The act rewrites the General Schedule pay table in 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) and adds supporting policy language, and nothing in the source body self-repeals the enactment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, definitions, findings, and committee powers.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the \"Federal Executive & Senior Leadership Pay Standardization Act of 2025\""; "The Committee shall have subpoena power"
provisions.notes: Support material only.

provisions.text_summary: Replaces the General Schedule table in 5 U.S.C. 5332(a).
provisions.exact_change: Strike the existing General Schedule and insert the enacted GS-1 through GS-15 pay table.
provisions.evidence: "Section 5332(a) of title 5, United States Code, is amended by striking the General Schedule and inserting the following"
provisions.notes: This is the operative textual amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Aligns positions with General Schedule grades.
provisions.evidence: "The following positions, or equivalent positions, shall be compensated according to the General Schedule"
provisions.notes: Programmatic compensation guidance.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note at Title 5 section 101 already reflects the 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) baseline incorporation; the remaining issue is the Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain the existing baseline incorporation of the 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) pay-table rewrite at /us/usc/t5/s101.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 32-231 | District Improvement Act (DIA)
laws.status_basis: The chronology seed lists PL 36-260 as repealing PL 30-223 and PL 32-231, so this enactment is retained only as statutory history.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and submission authority.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be referred to by its short title, the \"District Improvement Act\""; "This bill shall be effective immediately"
provisions.notes: Historical only after later repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals prior public laws and related local-law provisions.
provisions.target: Public Law 25-181; Public Law 15-105; Public Law 29-216 section 402(iv)
provisions.exact_change: Treat the listed repeals as historical enactment material only.
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 25-181 ... shall be repealed"; "Public Law 15-105 ... shall be repealed"; "Section 402 (iv) of Public Law 29-216"
provisions.notes: The later repeal makes these provisions historical.

provisions.text_summary: Establishes Special Prosecutors for the District and allocates local authority.
provisions.evidence: "There shall be established \"Special Prosecutors for the District\""
provisions.notes: Local-district governance only; preserved as history.

provisions.text_summary: Amends the District inspector-general structure through PL 28-205.
provisions.exact_change: Update the referenced inspector-general language as part of the now-historical act.
provisions.evidence: "Section 5 (b)(i) of Public Law 28-205"
provisions.notes: Historical only after repeal by later law.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is in a reasonable historical-note posture for a later-repealed District law, but it still embeds a full quotedContent dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Retain only a concise historical note at /us/usc/t50/s1.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
